---
title: "Storing API Keys with Keyring"
date: "2026-03-17"
date-modified: "2026-03-17"
version: "1.0"
description: "How to securely store and retrieve API keys using the Python keyring package."
categories: [python, api, security, workflow]
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
---

# Overview

API keys must never be hardcoded in scripts or committed to Git. This vignette covers how to store API keys in the system keyring — a secure, OS-managed credential store — and retrieve them at runtime for use with the OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.

For how to use a retrieved key in a conversation workflow, see `vignettes/python-and-chatlas.qmd`.

---

# Install Keyring

If keyring is not already installed in your environment:

```bash
uv add keyring
```

Or, if using a standard venv:

```bash
pip install keyring
```

---

# Store a Key (One-Time Setup)

Run this once per machine per API key. It stores the key in the OS credential store (Keychain on macOS, Credential Manager on Windows, libsecret on Linux). You do not need to re-run this unless the key changes.

```python
import keyring

keyring.set_password("service-name", "username", "your-api-key-here")
```

**Service names used in this repo:**

| Provider  | Service name                  | Username |
|-----------|-------------------------------|----------|
| OpenAI    | `bradGPT`                     | `brad`   |
| Anthropic | `claude-my-ai-assistant`      | `brad`   |

For example, to store your OpenAI key:

```python
import keyring

keyring.set_password("bradGPT", "brad", "sk-...")
```

And to store your Anthropic key:

```python
import keyring

keyring.set_password("claude-my-ai-assistant", "brad", "sk-ant-...")
```

::: callout-important
Run these in a scratch script or an interactive Python session — never in a file that gets committed to Git, since the key value will be visible in the source.
:::

---

# Retrieve a Key at Runtime

The standard pattern in this repo retrieves the key from keyring and sets it as a temporary environment variable for the duration of the session:

```python
import keyring
import os

# OpenAI
openai_api = keyring.get_password("bradGPT", "brad")
if openai_api is not None:
    os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY'] = openai_api
    print("OPENAI_API_KEY set.")
else:
    print("Could not retrieve OpenAI key from keyring.")

# Anthropic
claude_api = keyring.get_password("claude-my-ai-assistant", "brad")
if claude_api is not None:
    os.environ['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = claude_api
    print("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set.")
else:
    print("Could not retrieve Anthropic key from keyring.")
```

The API client libraries (`openai`, `anthropic`, `chatlas`) automatically read from these environment variables, so no further configuration is needed.

---

# Update or Delete a Key

To update a key, call `set_password` again with the new value — it overwrites the existing entry:

```python
keyring.set_password("bradGPT", "brad", "sk-new-key-here")
```

To delete a key entirely:

```python
keyring.delete_password("bradGPT", "brad")
```

---

# Quick Reference

| Action        | Command                                                      |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| Store a key   | `keyring.set_password("service", "username", "key")`         |
| Retrieve a key| `keyring.get_password("service", "username")`                |
| Delete a key  | `keyring.delete_password("service", "username")`             |

| Provider  | Service name             | Username |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------|
| OpenAI    | `bradGPT`                | `brad`   |
| Anthropic | `claude-my-ai-assistant` | `brad`   |
